title: Burden of disease
subtitle: [Disability-Adjusted Life Years](#dod:dalys) (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals from all causes. DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.
caption:
Burden of disease, 2021
Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals from all causes. DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of the total burden of disease in 2021, measured in Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals. The visualization utilizes a continuous color scale ranging from light beige to deep maroon to represent the magnitude of health loss, where darker shades correspond to higher DALY rates. The legend defines the scale starting at 0 and extending through intervals of 10,000 up to 60,000 DALYs per 100,000 people, with hatched grey areas indicating regions where no data is available. The data shows a distinct geographic concentration of high disease burden across the African continent. Countries in sub-Saharan Africa, including Chad, the Central African Republic, Nigeria, and Somalia, appear in the darkest maroon shades, placing them in the highest bracket of over 50,000 to 60,000 DALYs per 100,000 individuals. In contrast, the map shows significantly lower disease burdens across high-income regions. North America, Western Europe, and Australia are shaded in lighter beige and orange tones, corresponding to values generally falling between 10,000 and 30,000 DALYs per 100,000 people. Russia and parts of Eastern Europe display intermediate to high values, appearing in darker orange and red shades that suggest rates between 30,000 and 50,000. South American nations largely occupy the middle of the spectrum, with colors indicating rates roughly between 20,000 and 40,000. Greenland is marked with the hatched pattern indicating no data. The metric is age-standardized to allow for comparisons between countries. The visual pattern establishes a clear divide, with the highest rates of years lost to premature death and disability concentrated in central and western Africa, while the lowest rates are visible across the Americas, Europe, and Oceania.
